exercises.name: Sandbag Alternating Curtsy Lunge
exercises.description: Alternate curtsy lunges while holding a sandbag at your chest.

exercises.name: Sandbag Alternating Forward Lunge
exercises.description: Hold a sandbag. Alternate forward lunges while keeping your core tight.

exercises.name: Sandbag Alternating Lunge with Rotation
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, as you step back into reverse lunge, you’ll want to make sure that the front knee doesn’t track very far over top of your toes.

2) Try to keep your chest tall as the sandbag rotates to the side where the leg will be in front.

exercises.name: Sandbag Alternating Swings
exercises.description: Perform sandbag swings, alternating the hand holding the bag at the top of each swing.

exercises.name: Sandbag Bear Crawl Drag
exercises.description: Bear crawl forward while dragging a sandbag between your hands with alternating pulls.

exercises.name: Sandbag Bent Over Row
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll want to maintain a nice set Deadlift position so your back is an extension bent forward from the hips.

2) From there, when you row, squeeze between the shoulder blades while you’re performing the row itself.

exercises.name: Sandbag Bicep Curl
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll want to ensure that your elbow stays isolated and in a single position as you articulate at the elbow drive.

2) Begin by holding the sandbag in front of you.

3) Drive upwards to your chest, then back to starting position."

exercises.name: Sandbag Curtsy Lunge
exercises.description: Step one leg diagonally behind the other while holding a sandbag. Return to start and alternate sides.

exercises.name: Sandbag Deadlift
exercises.description: Stand over a sandbag. Hinge at the hips to grab it, then stand tall, squeezing your glutes.

exercises.name: Sandbag Drag Bear Plank
exercises.description: Start in a bear plank. Drag the sandbag with one hand across your body, keeping hips stable. Alternate sides.

exercises.name: Sandbag Drag with Push Up
exercises.description: Perform a push-up, then drag a sandbag across your body with one hand. Repeat, alternating sides.

exercises.name: Sandbag Forward Lunge
exercises.description: Hold a sandbag at your chest. Step forward into a lunge and push back to start. Alternate legs.

exercises.name: Sandbag Front Squat
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll need to clean the sandbag up to get going.

2) From there, you’ll want to ensure proper squat form, that means knees slightly press laterally as well as tracking your second toe and your hip.

3) You’ll also need to tall throughout your thoracic spine that means elbows up, chest up.

4) Carry the sandbag in a front rack position. Then begin your squat.

5) Perform certain number of repetitions."

exercises.name: Sandbag Full Clean
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll want to maintain a setback or a slight extension.

2) Your primary force is going to be driven from your lower body so use your hips to thrust forward as you put momentum into the sandbag.

3) When you scoop and catch the sandbag, you’ll put it into a front rack position pressing on your shoulders and then return it."

exercises.name: Sandbag Good Morning
exercises.description: Hold a sandbag on your back. Hinge at the hips, lowering your torso, then return to standing.

exercises.name: Sandbag Half Moon
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll want to ensure as close to a good squat form as possible maintaining a strong upper back, and slight extension pattern  and lowering from the hips and the knees.

2) From there, as you extend up overhead, you’ll pivot through your legs.

exercises.name: Sandbag High Plank Drags
exercises.description: In a plank position, drag a sandbag across your body with one hand while stabilizing with the other.

exercises.name: Sandbag Lunge into Single Leg Dead Lift
exercises.description: Lunge back holding a sandbag, then transition into a single-leg deadlift. Repeat on the other leg.

exercises.name: Sandbag Overhead Chop
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, the first phase of the movement will be a clean.

2) Keep extension through the back, drive up through the legs and the hips.

3) From there, the momentum will carry up overhead and you’ll catch in an overhead triceps extension.

4) Then reverse our movement.

exercises.name: Sandbag Overhead Shoulder Press
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll want to ensure a tall and set back as you pull the sandbag up into the front rack position.

2) From there, you’ll want to ensure that you’re holding on to the wide grip.

3) As you press up, fully extend overhead so that your arms are in line laterally with your ears.

4. Perform certain number of repetitions.

exercises.name: Sandbag Overhead Squat
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll want to ensure that you’ll clean the sandbag up with a nice set back.

2) Then extend overhead before you perform the squat.

3) While performing the squat, you’ll want to ensure that you have your feet set slightly wider than hip-foot apart.

4) Sit back and make sure that you keep your chest tall and open up your chest in order to maintain the sandbag overhead as you go into your squat.

exercises.name: Sandbag Overhead Tricep Extension
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, try to isolate your upper arm in a single position pointing straight up overhead.

2) From there, you’ll just articulate at the elbows to extend.

exercises.name: Sandbag Reverse Alternating Lunge
exercises.description: Hold a sandbag. Step back into alternating lunges while maintaining upright posture.

exercises.name: Sandbag Reverse Lunge
exercises.description: Hold a sandbag at your chest. Step back into a lunge and return to standing. Alternate legs.

exercises.name: Sandbag Shoulder Squat
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll need to get down into full squat and keep your chest high to get the sandbag up to your shoulder just to begin the exercise.

2) From there, you’ll want to maintain proper squat form which means slightly lateral as well as tracking over your second toe and being in line with your hip. 

3) From there, stay tall through your thoracic.

exercises.name: Sandbag Shoulder to Shoulder Press
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll first need to get into a squat and keep the body tall as you get the sandbag to up to your shoulder first.

2) From there, you’re going to have a little bit of a pop through the legs as you press up overhead.

3) Alternate to the other shoulder ensuring that you keep your core engaged the whole time.

exercises.name: Sandbag Side Lunge
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll need to get down into full squat and keep your chest high to get the sandbag up to your shoulder just to begin the exercise.

2) From there, you’ll want to maintain proper squat form which means slightly lateral as well as tracking over your second toe and being in line with your hip. 

3) From there, stay tall through your thoracic.

4) Once you’ve completed the first part of the set on one shoulder, switch and finish the set on the other."

exercises.name: Sandbag Single Arm Shouldering
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll use one arm, keep your chest tall and drive up to your leg to bring the sandbag from the ground to your shoulder.

2) Once you’ve completed the set with one arm, repeat it and finish the rest of the set on the other side.

exercises.name: Sandbag Single Arm Swings
exercises.description: Hold a sandbag with both hands. Swing it between your legs and up to chest height, engaging your hips.

exercises.name: Sandbag Single Leg Deadlift
exercises.description: Stand over a sandbag. Hinge at the hips to grab it, then stand tall, squeezing your glutes.

exercises.name: Sandbag Single Leg Deadlift & Row
exercises.description: Perform a single leg deadlift while rowing the sandbag toward your chest. Return to start.

exercises.name: Sandbag Split Jerk
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll want to ensure that you clean the sandbag up safely first that means keeping the side extension through your thoracic spine.

2) From there, you’re going to put a slight upward momentum into the sandbag with your lower body. 

3) Then dropping down with your lower body and locking out with your arms overhead in line with your ears to complete the movement.

exercises.name: Sandbag Squat to Press
exercises.description: Hold a sandbag. Squat down and press the bag overhead as you return to standing.

exercises.name: Sandbag Step Up
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, to first load the sandbag, you’ll need to safely clean it up.

2) Then, shoulder press over so that you have the sandbag loaded on to your back.

3) From there, you’ll step up. While doing so, ensure that your knees are in line with your second toe and your hip.

4) When you’re done one side, perform the rest of the set on the other foot.

exercises.name: Sandbag Thrusters
exercises.description: Hold a sandbag at your chest. Perform a squat, then press the bag overhead as you rise.
